docs.title: Home
docs.text: Telecom and IT-oriented icon font.

docs.title: Source files / scripts
docs.text: All source files, scripts, and tools (excluding compiled fonts) are licensed under the BSD 3-Clause License. You are free to use, modify, and redistribute these files. Contributions are welcome, but official font binaries are generated only by Gufo Labs.

docs.title: Compiled fonts (woff2)
docs.text:
Official compiled fonts in webfonts/ are licensed under CC BY-ND 4.0.

  • Free to use, including commercially
  • Modifications or redistribution of modified fonts are not allowed

This ensures all official releases are centralized and controlled.

docs.title: On Gufo Stack
docs.text:
This product is a part of Gufo Stack - the collaborative effort led by Gufo Labs. Our goal is to create a robust and flexible set of tools to create network management software and automate routine administration tasks.

To do this, we extract the key technologies that have proven themselves in the NOC and bring them as separate packages. Then we work on API, performance tuning, documentation, and testing. The NOC uses the final result as the external dependencies.

Gufo Stack makes the NOC better, and this is our primary task. But other products can benefit from Gufo Stack too. So we believe that our effort will make the other network management products better.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file. The format is based on Keep a Changelog and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

To see unreleased changes, please see the CHANGELOG on the master branch guide.

docs.title: Added
docs.text: • Initial implementation.

docs.title: Icons
docs.text: Select icon...

docs.title: Using Stencils
docs.text:
To add a network node use <i> tag with the required classes:

<i class="gf cisco-router"></i>

Where:

  • gf - Enables Gufo Font.
  • cisco-router - The icon name (see the icon list for details).

Rendered result:

docs.title: Colour States
docs.text: Apply a state class to indicate the network node’s status.

docs.title: gf-unknown
docs.text:
Marks the node as being in an unknown state.

Example:

<i class="gf gf-unknown cisco-router"></i>

Rendered result:

docs.title: gf-ok
docs.text:
Indicates the node is up and operational.

Example:

<i class="gf gf-ok cisco-router"></i>

Rendered result:

docs.title: gf-warn
docs.text:
Indicates the node has active alarms or warnings.

Example:

<i class="gf gf-warn cisco-router"></i>

Rendered result:

docs.title: gf-fail
docs.text:
Marks the node as in a critical failure state.

Example:

<i class="gf gf-fail cisco-router"></i>

Rendered result:

docs.title: Glyph Size
docs.text: Default network node size is 64x64px. You can adjust it using "scale classes".

docs.title: Default
docs.text: Default size (64x64px). Rendered result:

docs.title: gf-2x
docs.text:
Doubles icon size.

Example:

<i class="gf gf-2x cisco-router"></i>

Rendered result:

docs.title: gf-3x
docs.text:
Triples icon size.

Example:

<i class="gf gf-3x cisco-router"></i>

Rendered result:

docs.title: Exact sizes
docs.text:
You can also set an exact icon size using pixel-based classes:

gf-16px gf-24px gf-32px gf-48px Default
